subject: LU-3373 osd-ldiskfs: Use ext4_map_blocks for IO map

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

messages.message:
Patch Set 1:

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/19152/

Patch Set 1: Fails

Build Failed 
 
http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/19152/ : FAILURE
Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 2:

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/19153/

Patch Set 2: Fails

Build Failed 
 
http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/19153/ : FAILURE

Patch Set 2: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

I think this patch needs to be dependent on http://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/7794 in order to build OK.

Patch Set 2:

I agree with bob this patch needs the dependency. I have tried it works

Patch Set 2: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Bob actually tried the patch out by itself. Actually it can be used by itself. I did noticed that their is no test for ext4_map_blocks in the autoconf system which is why I gave it a negative review. Also the patch needs to be rebased. ldiskfs_ext_new_extent_cb now calls ldiskfs_journal_start as well.

Patch Set 2:

ext4_map_blocks need export by ldiskfs patch. So I don't think need check it in autoconf. Just define the macro in ldiskfs patch.

Patch Set 2:

I see what you mean. We can replace all the old code by using ext4_map_block directly. We just have to add a export for ext4_map_block to all the ext4-misc.patch files.

Patch Set 2:

Okay just went through the code and I discovered ext4_map_blocks() doesn't exist for rhel6. We have it for SLES11SP[2/3] but not RHEL6 distros. Back porting ext4_map_blocks() would be really big patch. At this point I think testing for ext4_map_blocks() would be the better choice. Also for this patch to compile on rhel6 you need to add EXT4_HT_MISC to ext4-journal-callback.patch.

Patch Set 2:

Never mind I see this patch depends on 7794. Bob you were right.
Uploaded patch set 3.

Patch Set 3:

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/19547/

Patch Set 3: Verified

Build Successful 
 
http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/19547/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 3:

Still missing the test to see if ext4_map_blocks() is available. I have a version of this patch that has the test. If you don't mind I can push that patch. I did test the code using ext4_map_block() and haven't seen any problems.
Uploaded patch set 4.

Patch Set 5:

Yang Sheng, in the future when you are abandoning a patch it is useful to include a comment about why the patch was abandoned.  If it is replaced by another patch, please add the URL of that patch.

Otherwise, I found a comment in LU-3675 pointing to this patch as being needed for the 3.10 kernel, but it is abandoned without any way to know which patch (if any) replaced it.

Patch Set 5:

Hi, Andreas, Since i have talked Alex and FanYong. They are worry this patch maybe hurt performance than old implement. So i close this one first and intend to resubmit after do some test compare the performance. Anyway, I restore it for now. As the discuss can be continue.
Restored

Patch Set 5:

(2 comments)

2 style warning(s).
For more details please see https://wiki.hpdd.intel.com/display/PUB/Coding+Guidelines

Patch Set 5:

Jeff has also submitted a very similar patch

http://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/10163

Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

there is a newer patch in LU-4416 ?

Patch Set 5:

Hi, Alex, I just doing the test between walk_space & map_blocks to compare how much performance different. And will update patch later.

Patch Set 5:

I have done some performance compare test against walk_space & map_blocks interface. test patch link: http://review.whamcloud.com/10367 .
The test results show not so many difference between them. I just mainly test the read operation with a big file consist of 10688 extents. In theory, The map_blocks should lost performance by noncontinuous blocks. But i cannot see any big difference exist. Maybe i lost some things. Any advice appreciate.

Patch Set 5:

credits calculation makes no sense at this point. rather than confuse the reader, it would be better to grab a handle specifying "1" as credits and explain that we're already have a transaction started and where credits were calculated.
Uploaded patch set 6.

Patch Set 6:

Can you rebase this on top of http://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/10164.

Patch Set 7: Code-Review-1

Since SLES11 SP3 ext4 contains ext4_map_blocks this will impact it. You need Jeff's export-ext4-3.0.patch changes from http://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/10163 otherwise osd-ldiskfs will not load on SLES11 SP3.

Patch Set 7:

What do you means James? I think ldiskfs patches should commit in separate patch. Every distro should export ext4_map_blocks if want to use it. It is not this patch should include but ldiskfs patch. Please reconsider your review-. I'll commit ldiskfs patches if needed.

Patch Set 7:

Sorry, Almost forgot sles11 support. I think it better still use walk_space interface. Since it exist in sles11 sp3.

parents.subject: LU-5442 lustre: always include libcfs.h in lustre_idl.h

commit.subject: LU-3373 osd-ldiskfs: Use ext4_map_blocks for IO map
commit.message:
LU-3373 osd-ldiskfs: Use ext4_map_blocks for IO map

Use ext4_map_blocks instead patch ext4 code too much.

Signed-off-by: yang sheng <yang.sheng@intel.com>
Change-Id: Ifc0cf2e45fbcbff688c57d4da52e17471bd55386
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/8116
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@gmail.com>
Reviewed-by: Fan Yong <fan.yong@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
